dialogos.texto_en: Here we are.\nWell, come on in.
...Hm?\nIs something wrong?
I-I'm just nervous\nbecause it's my first time entering a boy's room...
Well, it's my first time\nhaving a girl in my room too.
You can't just stand in the entrance forever,\nyou know?
R-right.\nWell then, e-excuse me.
It's a small place,\nbut please have a seat wherever you like.
This is Futaro's room...
Hey, stop staring.\nIt's making me uncomfortable.
Teehee, it smells like you in here, Futaro.
Well, it's my room, so that's natural, right?
Anyway, give me the shopping bags.\nI'll put them in the kitchen.
Ah, okay.
You seemed quite used to doing the shopping.\nIt surprised me.
I do cook for myself, you know?\nNaturally, I keep track of all the special offers\nat the nearby supermarket!
Living alone is expensive,\nso I don't hesitate to put in the effort to save money!
Hehe, I see.
Well, with that being said,\nI would appreciate your help...
Once we catch our breath,\ncan I ask for your assistance right away?
Sure, okay.
L-like this?
No.\nHold the knife like this.
...Like this. Got it.
Ideally, you should adjust your grip\naccording to the ingredients you're cutting.
However, in your case, Futaro,\nI think the most orthodox grip is good enough.
If you remember this grip properly,\nyou'll be able to handle most of the ingredients\nyou regularly use.
Is that so?\nAs expected, going to culinary school\nmakes a difference.
I learned these basics from Nino, actually.
I see.\nSo your cooking foundation\nis thanks to Nino's guidance, huh?
Today, I'll be the teacher,\nand you'll be the student.\nI'm gonna be tough on you.
Yes, please!
Alright, next, let's cut the vegetables%p-2;――
Use your left hand like a cat's paw.
...A cat?
Gimme your left hand.
Huh?\nA-aaahh...
Curl your fingers lightly like this,\nmaking sure the thumb doesn't stick out\nmore than the other fingers.
Hold the side of the knife\nagainst the first joint of your\nmiddle and index fingers while cutting.
Ah, I see. This way, the blade won't hit my fingers,\nand I won't accidentally cut myself.
Then, use the first joint of your middle finger\nto determine the position of the blade%p-2;――
Yeah, you're doing well.\nIt's easy when you have a fast-learning student.
All thanks to you, Miku.
It's not just that.\nI only taught you the basics.
But, I'll protect your fingers like this,\nso go ahead and chop all the vegetables.
You got it!
So, what kind of studies are you doing at school?
Other than cooking, we study nutrition,\nfood science, and hygiene.
Wow, as expected of a vocational school.\nThose are all fields that are beyond me.
There's also management curriculum and so on.
I see. You're well on your way to fulfilling your dreams.
...Yeah.
When I had told you that I wasn't going to university, \nFutaro, your face looked really perplexed.
Eh...\nDid I make such a face?
Yeah, you did.
...Well, if I say I wasn't shocked,\nI'd be lying.
But, it's the path you decided to take.\nIt's not my place to say anything about it.
Yeah...\nThat's why I wanted to tell you today how far I've come.
It's already enough, I understand.
Wow, amazing! The meat and vegetable stir-fry\nis complete! It's a proper dish!
Well...\nYou said you cook for yourself, so I was worried\nabout what kind of dishes you were making...
Don't worry about it.\nNow that we've plated it perfectly,\nlet's eat it while it's still warm.
Leeet's eeaat!
Let's eat.
Ohh...!\nThe seasoning is just right!\nI can't stop eating.
What about you, Miku?\nAs a cook, how does it fare?
...I want you to feed me.
Huh?
I want you to f-feed me, please.\nThat will be my tuition fee for today's lesson.
No, I didn't agree to that.
You said I can be as selfish as I want, right?\nWas that perhaps a lie?
Ooh...!\nFine, but only once, okay?
Okay.
So... Here we go, then?
Say, aah...
Aah...
Nom... Nom...
And? How's the taste?
Teehee, I don't know.
What? What do you mean?
I'm so happy and embarrassed at the same time\nthat I don't know how it tastes.
Hey, hey...
I might know if I eat it again.\nHere. Aaah.
*sigh*\nThere's really nothing to be done with you, Miku.\nYet, I'll stick with you to the end.
